title: Add Monthly Reports Page

requirements.text: Add a backend method `getDeliveryRecordsByMonth(month: Nat, year: Nat)` to the main Motoko actor that returns all DeliveryRecord entries whose date falls within the specified month and year. Also add a backend method `getMilkRecordsByMonth(month: Nat, year: Nat)` that returns all milk production records for the specified month and year.

quotes: add monthly report page with all of the above

acceptanceCriteria: getDeliveryRecordsByMonth returns only records whose date matches the given month and year
getMilkRecordsByMonth returns only milk production records matching the given month and year
Both methods are exposed as query or update calls on the main actor

requirements.text: Add React Query hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` for the two new backend methods: one to fetch delivery records by month/year and one to fetch milk production records by month/year.

quotes: add monthly report page with all of the above

acceptanceCriteria: A hook exists for fetching delivery records filtered by month and year
A hook exists for fetching milk production records filtered by month and year
Hooks use the existing actor pattern from useQueries.ts

requirements.text: Create a new Monthly Reports page at route `/reports` in `frontend/src/App.tsx`. The page must include a month/year selector (defaulting to the current month and year). It must contain two sections: (1) Monthly Delivery Report and (2) Monthly Milk Production Report.

quotes: add monthly report page with all of the above

acceptanceCriteria: Route /reports renders the Monthly Reports page
Page has a month and year selector defaulting to the current month/year
Changing the month/year selector refreshes all report data on the page
Page is wrapped in the existing Layout component and uses the farm-themed card-based design

requirements.text: In the Monthly Delivery Report section of the Monthly Reports page, display: (1) Summary totals card showing total deliveries count, total missed deliveries count, and total liters delivered for the selected month. (2) A per-customer breakdown table showing each customer's name, total deliveries received, total missed, and total liters. (3) A per-delivery-boy performance table showing each delivery boy's name, total deliveries made, total missed, and total liters delivered.

quotes: Summary totals: total deliveries, total missed, total liters delivered
Breakdown per customer (total received vs missed)

acceptanceCriteria: Summary totals card shows correct aggregated counts for the selected month
Per-customer breakdown table shows one row per customer with their delivery stats
Per-delivery-boy table shows one row per delivery boy with their performance stats
All data reflects the selected month and year

requirements.text: In the Monthly Milk Production Report section of the Monthly Reports page, display: (1) A summary card showing total liters produced for the selected month. (2) A per-cattle breakdown table showing each cattle's name/ID and total liters produced that month.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary card shows total milk production in liters for the selected month
Per-cattle breakdown table shows one row per cattle with total liters for the month
Data updates when the month/year selector changes

requirements.text: Add a print-friendly view to the Monthly Reports page. Include a 'Print Report' button that triggers `window.print()`. Apply print-specific CSS so that the navigation bar, month/year selector, and action buttons are hidden when printing, and the report content (both delivery and milk production sections) is rendered cleanly in black and white for printing.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Print Report' button is visible on the page
Clicking the button triggers the browser print dialog
When printing, navigation bar and interactive controls are hidden
Report tables and summary cards are fully visible and legible in the printed output

requirements.text: Add a 'Reports' navigation link to the existing Layout component's top navigation bar (both desktop and mobile hamburger menu variants), pointing to the `/reports` route. Position it alongside the existing Dashboard, Cattle, Milk Production, Inventory, Customers, and Deliveries links.

quotes: add monthly report page with all of the above

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Reports' link appears in the desktop navigation bar
A 'Reports' link appears in the mobile hamburger menu
Clicking the link navigates to /reports
Active state styling matches the existing nav link pattern

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo as a single actor
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
New pages must use the existing farm-themed design system (warm greens and ambers, card-based layout) consistent with all other pages
nonGoals: Sending notifications (SMS, email, push) to customers
Exporting reports to PDF or CSV files
Adding new cattle or delivery data from within the reports page
Real-time data updates or WebSocket integration
